title: How to Clean Silicone Right

hook_slide.headline: STOP! You're Cleaning Silicone Incorrectly.
hook_slide.subline: Many common cleaning methods can actually damage your favorite silicone items.
hook_slide.image_concept: Dark mood, warm minimal lighting, a textured silicone object in soft focus, hinting at a hidden problem.

education_slides.headline: Beyond Just Clean
education_slides.body: Proper cleaning isn't just about appearance; it's vital for hygiene, preventing bacterial growth, and extending the lifespan of your cherished silicone items.
education_slides.image_concept: Macro shot of smooth, pristine silicone texture, inviting and clean.

education_slides.headline: The Gentle Power Duo
education_slides.body: For everyday cleaning, warm water and a mild, fragrance-free soap are your best friends. Avoid harsh chemicals that can degrade silicone.
education_slides.image_concept: Minimalist shot of a bar of mild, unscented soap next to a gentle stream of warm water.

education_slides.headline: Wash, Rinse, Repeat
education_slides.body: Lather gently, ensuring all surfaces are covered. Rinse thoroughly under running water until no soap residue remains, leaving no film.
education_slides.image_concept: Hands gently washing a smooth, unidentifiable silicone shape under a soft stream of water, clean and calm.

education_slides.headline: Air It Out Right
education_slides.body: Air drying on a clean, lint-free surface is ideal. If you must towel dry, use a dedicated lint-free cloth to prevent fibers from sticking.
education_slides.image_concept: A silicone object air-drying gracefully on a clean, soft, textured cloth in warm, natural light.

education_slides.headline: The Don't List
education_slides.body: Steer clear of alcohol, bleach, petroleum-based cleaners, and dishwashers. These can damage silicone, making it sticky or porous over time.
education_slides.image_concept: A subtle 'stop' sign graphic overlay on a blurred background of generic cleaning products, emphasizing caution.

education_slides.headline: When to Go Deeper
education_slides.body: For deeper sanitization, some silicone items can be briefly boiled or sterilized with a dedicated UV sanitizer. Always check manufacturer instructions first.
education_slides.image_concept: A subtle steam effect over a clean silicone object, or a soft glow hinting at UV sterilization, clean and scientific.

education_slides.headline: Storage Secrets
education_slides.body: Store clean, dry silicone separately from other materials like rubber or jelly-based items to prevent degradation and cross-contamination.
education_slides.image_concept: A neatly organized drawer or box with soft, fabric dividers, containing smooth, clean shapes, suggesting careful storage.

framework_slide.title: Your Quick Silicone Cleaning Checklist
items: Use warm water & mild, fragrance-free soap.
Air dry or use a dedicated lint-free cloth.
Consult manufacturer for deep cleaning guidelines.
framework_slide.image_concept: A clean, minimalist graphic with a checklist layout, warm background, and subtle texture, highly save-worthy.

cta_slide.headline: Curated by Dark Fantasy
cta_slide.image_concept: Minimalist Dark Fantasy logo on a warm, inviting, textured background, elegant and soft.

caption: Keep your silicone items pristine and safe! ✨ Proper care extends their life and ensures your wellness journey is always clean.
